content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 207 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • • Administration • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322 5662        322 5660        322 5901        322 6441        322 6444

March 19, 1979

Edmund G. Brown
450 N. Roxbury
Beverly Hills, CA 91020

Dear Governor Brown:

     This will confirm today's telephone conversation when I
advised you concerning a contribution for your daughter's
campaign.

     The facts as I understand them are as follows. A con-
tributor gave you a $200 check for Kathleen Brown Rice's
campaign for the board of Education. The check was made
payable to you. You asked whether you could endorse the
check over to the Citizens to Elect Kathleen Brown Rice. I
advised you that there would be no problem with that procedure
under the Political Reform Act.

     I also advised you that since the check contained the
name of the contributor as the payor and was earmarked by
the contributor as a contribution to Kathleen Brown Rice you
will not be an intermediary for the contribution by endorsing
the check over to Citizens to Elect Kathleen Brown Rice.

If you have any other questions concerning the Political
Reform Act, please feel free to call.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Barbara Campbell
                                        Barbara Campbell
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

BC:plh

cc: Citizens to Elect Kathleen
     Brown Rice
     Attention: Kathleen Brown Rice
     6022 Wilshire Blvd., Suite 201
     Los Angeles, CA 90036

sections.question_synthetic: Can Governor Brown endorse a $200 check made payable to him over to his daughter's campaign committee, and would he be considered an intermediary under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: There is no problem with endorsing the check over to the campaign committee. Governor Brown will not be considered an intermediary because the check is earmarked by the contributor for the daughter's campaign.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers but contains clear factual recitation and legal advice. Content and format are consistent with FPPC advice letters. Telephone consultation confirmed in writing.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Can Governor Brown endorse a $200 check made payable to him over to his daughter's campaign committee, and would he be considered an intermediary under the Political Reform Act?

CONCLUSION: There is no problem with endorsing the check over to the campaign committee. Governor Brown will not be considered an intermediary because the check is earmarked by the contributor for the daughter's campaign.

The facts as I understand them are as follows. A con- tributor gave you a $200 check for Kathleen Brown Rice's campaign for the board of Education. The check was made payable to you. You asked whether you could endorse the check over to the Citizens to Elect Kathleen Brown Rice. I advised you that there would be no problem with that procedure under the Political Reform Act. embedding.summary: FPPC counsel advises Governor Edmund Brown that he may endorse a $200 campaign contribution check to his daughter's campaign committee without being deemed an intermediary, since the check was earmarked by the contributor for that specific purpose.
